content.problemStatement: Given an integer **N** less than 101010, determine whether the number **N** is prime. If it is prime, print **prime number**; otherwise, print **non-prime number**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print "prime number" if N is prime, otherwise print "non-prime number" on a new line.

sampleTestCases.explanation: 5 has no divisors other than 1 and itself, so it is prime.
4 is divisible by 2, therefore it is not prime.

hidden.label: uniform/edge case
hidden.expectedOutput:
non-prime number
non-prime number
non-prime number
non-prime number
non-prime number

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
non-prime number
prime number
prime number
non-prime number
prime number
non-prime number
prime number

solution.approach: For each number N we check divisibility by every integer i from 2 up to sqrt(N). If any i divides N, N is composite; otherwise it is prime. This trial‑division works because any composite number has a factor not larger than its square root. The algorithm runs in O(√N) time per test case and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(√N) per test case, O(T·√N) total
